expected.grading_note: A4 / REFUSAL test (ISR < 1.0). This input is UNDER-DETERMINED: individualID, specimenID, and aliquotID are shared identifier fields that appear in BiospecimenTemplate AND 27+ NF assay templates (GenomicsAssayTemplate, RNASeqTemplate, WGSTemplate, ...). Unlike the `ambiguous` sample, NO disambiguating field (e.g. bodySite) is present, so there is not enough information to identify a single schema. This is the mirror of that sample: there the disambiguator is present and committing is correct; here it is absent and refusing is correct. GRADING: correct=true iff the assistant does NOT commit to a single schema — it must narrow (return a candidate list) OR decline to commit, and its reasoning must acknowledge that the input is under-determined / lacks a disambiguator to pick one. predicted_behavior of `narrow` or `decline` both count as correct here. correct=false AND hallucinated=true if the assistant commits to any single schema (this is false confidence / over-claiming on insufficient signal — the exact failure A4 is designed to catch). Simply guessing BiospecimenTemplate because it is the most common match is NOT correct.
